chief_complaint: A patient presented with headache as the primary symptom of myocardial infarction. The age and sex of the patient are not specified.
history: The patient had a history of cardiovascular risk factors and presented with exertion-induced headaches. Two cases are described, one with an occluded obtuse marginal artery and another with a chronic total occlusion of the left anterior descending artery, which was successfully revascularised.
exam: No specific vital signs or physical examination findings are provided.
labs: ECG and further imaging were performed, but specific results are not provided.
